Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-dropdown' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

### Fix 4
Lines: 47, 54
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-splititem-component]:
Component <SplitItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <SplitItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <SplitItem>{content} other children</SplitItem>
  After:  <SplitItem component=>other children</SplitItem>

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/catalog/templatescatalog/components/TemplatesCatalogFilters/TemplatesCatalogFiltersGroup.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
Let me start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix:

**Fixes 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7** — All target the same thing: the CSS class `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle-icon` on lines 49 and 51. This class (and its parent classes `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle` and `pf-v5-c-dropdown`) have been removed in PF v6 with no equivalent. The icons are being used as expand/collapse indicators in a custom filter group header — they're not inside a Dropdown component at all. The class was just being borrowed for styling the toggle icon. The fix is to remove the `className` prop entirely since the class no longer exists. The icons will still render; they just won't have the removed PF dropdown styling.

**Fix 4** — `SplitItem` has a new `component` prop. Looking at the two `SplitItem` usages:
- Line 47: Contains the expand/collapse icon — the children are conditional JSX icons. This is standard children content, not something that should move to `component`.
- Line 54: Contains a `Title` with `className="vm-filter-group-title"` and `isFilled`. Again, standard children usage.

The `component` prop on `SplitItem` in PF v6 is actually for specifying the HTML element type (like `div`, `span`, etc.), similar to other PF `component` props. It's not for absorbing children content. No change needed for this fix — the current usage is correct.

Let me apply the changes:
Now let me verify the final state of the file:

The file looks correct. All changes have been applied cleanly.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Removed `className="pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle-icon"` from `AngleDownIcon` (line 49) and `AngleRightIcon` (line 51). The CSS class `pf-v5-c-dropdown` was removed in PF v6 with no equivalent. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-toggle` | ✅ Applied | Same change as Fix 1 — the `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle-icon` class contained the removed `__toggle` segment. Resolved by the same className removal. |
| Fix 3 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-toggle-icon` | ✅ Applied | Same change as Fix 1 — the full class `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle-icon` was removed. Resolved by the same className removal. |
| Fix 4 | `sd-new-absorbing-prop-splititem-component` | ⚠️ No change needed | The `component` prop on `SplitItem` in PF v6 specifies the rendered HTML element type (e.g., `div`, `span`), not a slot for absorbing children content. Both `SplitItem` usages have standard children content (icons and a Title) that should remain as children. No migration needed. |
| Fix 5 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Same underlying issue as Fix 1 — resolved by the same className removal on both icon components. |
| Fix 6 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown-toggle` | ✅ Applied | Same underlying issue as Fix 2 — resolved by the same className removal. |
| Fix 7 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown-toggle-icon` | ✅ Applied | Same underlying issue as Fix 3 — resolved by the same className removal. |

**Summary:** Fixes 1–3 and 5–7 all targeted the same two lines (49, 51) where `className="pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle-icon"` was used on `AngleDownIcon` and `AngleRightIcon`. This class (and its parent BEM classes) were removed in PF v6 with no replacement. The className props were removed entirely — the icons are used as custom expand/collapse indicators in a filter group header and don't need PF dropdown styling. Fix 4 required no change as the `SplitItem` children usage is already correct.

chunks.response:
The file looks correct. All changes have been applied cleanly.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Removed `className="pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle-icon"` from `AngleDownIcon` (line 49) and `AngleRightIcon` (line 51). The CSS class `pf-v5-c-dropdown` was removed in PF v6 with no equivalent. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-toggle` | ✅ Applied | Same change as Fix 1 — the `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle-icon` class contained the removed `__toggle` segment. Resolved by the same className removal. |
| Fix 3 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-toggle-icon` | ✅ Applied | Same change as Fix 1 — the full class `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle-icon` was removed. Resolved by the same className removal. |
| Fix 4 | `sd-new-absorbing-prop-splititem-component` | ⚠️ No change needed | The `component` prop on `SplitItem` in PF v6 specifies the rendered HTML element type (e.g., `div`, `span`), not a slot for absorbing children content. Both `SplitItem` usages have standard children content (icons and a Title) that should remain as children. No migration needed. |
| Fix 5 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Same underlying issue as Fix 1 — resolved by the same className removal on both icon components. |
| Fix 6 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown-toggle` | ✅ Applied | Same underlying issue as Fix 2 — resolved by the same className removal. |
| Fix 7 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown-toggle-icon` | ✅ Applied | Same underlying issue as Fix 3 — resolved by the same className removal. |

**Summary:** Fixes 1–3 and 5–7 all targeted the same two lines (49, 51) where `className="pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle-icon"` was used on `AngleDownIcon` and `AngleRightIcon`. This class (and its parent BEM classes) were removed in PF v6 with no replacement. The className props were removed entirely — the icons are used as custom expand/collapse indicators in a filter group header and don't need PF dropdown styling. Fix 4 required no change as the `SplitItem` children usage is already correct.
